source_paper_title: Mirror Descent Algorithms for Risk Budgeting Portfolios
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Arnaiz_Iglesias_2026, title={Mirror Descent Algorithms for Risk Budgeting Portfolios}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2024.0847}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2024.0847}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Arnaiz Iglesias, Martín and Cetingoz, Adil Rengim and Frikha, Noufel}, year={2026}, month=apr }
context:
Conclusion (page 25 of 34)

Potential directions for future research include investigating asymptotic error properties by establishing a central limit theorem for the stochastic MD algorithm presented here.
title: Establish a central limit theorem for stochastic mirror descent risk budgeting portfolios
background_latex:
Let \(d\in\mathbb{N}\) be the number of assets and \(X\) an \(\mathbb{R}^d\)-valued random vector of asset returns on a probability space \((\Omega,\mathcal{F},\mathbb{P})\). For portfolio weights \(u\in\Delta_d:=\{u\in\mathbb{R}^d_+:\sum_{i=1}^d u_i=1\}\), the portfolio loss is \(-\langle u,X\rangle\).

Fix a risk-budget vector \(b\in\Delta^{>0}_d:=\{b\in(0,\infty)^d:\sum_{i=1}^d b_i=1\}\). Let \(\rho\) be an RB-compatible risk measure (positive homogeneous and subadditive) such that the map
\[r_\rho(y):=\rho(-\langle y,X\rangle),\qquad y\in\mathbb{R}^d_+\]
is continuous on \(\mathbb{R}^d_+\) and continuously differentiable on \((0,\infty)^d\). Let \(g:\mathbb{R}_+\to\mathbb{R}\) be continuously differentiable, convex, and increasing, and define the strictly convex objective
\[\Gamma_g(y):= g(r_\rho(y)) - \sum_{i=1}^d b_i\log y_i,\qquad y\in(0,\infty)^d.\]
Let \(y^\star\in(0,\infty)^d\) denote the unique minimizer of \(\Gamma_g\), and \(u^\star:=y^\star/\|y^\star\|_1\) the corresponding (unique) risk budgeting portfolio.

Assume a stochastic representation of \(g\circ r_\rho\): there exists a convex loss function \(L:\mathbb{R}^2\to\mathbb{R}\) such that for all \(y\in(0,\infty)^d\),
\[g(r_\rho(y)) = \min_{\xi\in\mathbb{R}} \mathbb{E}[L(\xi,-\langle y,X\rangle)] = \mathbb{E}[L(\xi^\star(y),-\langle y,X\rangle)],\]
where \(\xi^\star(y)\) is uniquely defined. Define the joint variable \(z=(\xi,y)\in\mathbb{R}\times(0,\infty)^d\), and the population objective
\[h(z):=\mathbb{E}[H(z,X)],\qquad H(z,X):=L(\xi,-\langle y,X\rangle) - \sum_{i=1}^d b_i\log y_i.\]
Let \(z^\star=(\xi^\star,y^\star)\) be the unique minimizer of \(h\).

Because \(\nabla_y H\) is singular as some \(y_i\downarrow 0\), the paper introduces a tamed factor \(\kappa(y):= (\min_{1\le i\le d} y_i)\wedge 1\). Using i.i.d. samples \((X_k)_{k\ge 1}\), a stochastic mirror descent (SMD) recursion is defined for a chosen radius \(m>0\) (assumed \(m\ge \|y^\star\|_1\)) and step sizes \((\gamma_k)\):
1) \(\xi_{k+1} = \xi_k - \gamma_{k+1}\,\partial_\xi H(z_k,X_{k+1})\).
2) \(y_{k+1} = P^{m}_{y_k}\bigl(\gamma_{k+1}\,\kappa(y_k)\,\nabla_y H(z_k,X_{k+1})\bigr)\),
where \(P^{m}_{y}(v)\) is the negative-entropy mirror (KL) proximal map onto \((0,\infty)^d\cap\{\|y\|_1\le m\}\) (explicitly \(y\mapsto y\odot e^{-v}\) followed by \(\ell_1\)-rescaling if needed).
The paper proves almost sure convergence \(z_k\to z^\star\) and provides an almost sure non-asymptotic bound for the weighted averaged iterates \(\bar z_n := (\sum_{k=1}^n \gamma_k z_{k-1})/(\sum_{k=1}^n \gamma_k)\).
problem_definition_latex:
Under suitable regularity and moment assumptions ensuring the SMD recursion \((z_k)_{k\ge 0}\) is well-defined and converges to \(z^\star\), derive an asymptotic distributional error characterization for the stochastic mirror descent risk-budgeting estimator.

Concretely, establish a central limit theorem (CLT) for an appropriately normalized error of either the raw iterates \(z_n\) or the averaged iterates \(\bar z_n\), i.e. prove convergence in distribution of the form
\[a_n\,(\bar z_n - z^\star) \;\Rightarrow\; \mathcal{N}(0,\Sigma),\]
for a deterministic scaling \(a_n\to\infty\) and a covariance matrix \(\Sigma\), and characterize \(a_n\) and \(\Sigma\) in terms of the model primitives \((L,X,b,g,\rho)\) and the mirror map/taming mechanism \((\kappa, P_y^m)\).
latex:
Let \(d\in\mathbb{N}\) be the number of assets and \(X\) an \(\mathbb{R}^d\)-valued random vector of asset returns on a probability space \((\Omega,\mathcal{F},\mathbb{P})\). For portfolio weights \(u\in\Delta_d:=\{u\in\mathbb{R}^d_+:\sum_{i=1}^d u_i=1\}\), the portfolio loss is \(-\langle u,X\rangle\).

Fix a risk-budget vector \(b\in\Delta^{>0}_d:=\{b\in(0,\infty)^d:\sum_{i=1}^d b_i=1\}\). Let \(\rho\) be an RB-compatible risk measure (positive homogeneous and subadditive) such that the map
\[r_\rho(y):=\rho(-\langle y,X\rangle),\qquad y\in\mathbb{R}^d_+\]
is continuous on \(\mathbb{R}^d_+\) and continuously differentiable on \((0,\infty)^d\). Let \(g:\mathbb{R}_+\to\mathbb{R}\) be continuously differentiable, convex, and increasing, and define the strictly convex objective
\[\Gamma_g(y):= g(r_\rho(y)) - \sum_{i=1}^d b_i\log y_i,\qquad y\in(0,\infty)^d.\]
Let \(y^\star\in(0,\infty)^d\) denote the unique minimizer of \(\Gamma_g\), and \(u^\star:=y^\star/\|y^\star\|_1\) the corresponding (unique) risk budgeting portfolio.

Assume a stochastic representation of \(g\circ r_\rho\): there exists a convex loss function \(L:\mathbb{R}^2\to\mathbb{R}\) such that for all \(y\in(0,\infty)^d\),
\[g(r_\rho(y)) = \min_{\xi\in\mathbb{R}} \mathbb{E}[L(\xi,-\langle y,X\rangle)] = \mathbb{E}[L(\xi^\star(y),-\langle y,X\rangle)],\]
where \(\xi^\star(y)\) is uniquely defined. Define the joint variable \(z=(\xi,y)\in\mathbb{R}\times(0,\infty)^d\), and the population objective
\[h(z):=\mathbb{E}[H(z,X)],\qquad H(z,X):=L(\xi,-\langle y,X\rangle) - \sum_{i=1}^d b_i\log y_i.\]
Let \(z^\star=(\xi^\star,y^\star)\) be the unique minimizer of \(h\).

Because \(\nabla_y H\) is singular as some \(y_i\downarrow 0\), the paper introduces a tamed factor \(\kappa(y):= (\min_{1\le i\le d} y_i)\wedge 1\). Using i.i.d. samples \((X_k)_{k\ge 1}\), a stochastic mirror descent (SMD) recursion is defined for a chosen radius \(m>0\) (assumed \(m\ge \|y^\star\|_1\)) and step sizes \((\gamma_k)\):
1) \(\xi_{k+1} = \xi_k - \gamma_{k+1}\,\partial_\xi H(z_k,X_{k+1})\).
2) \(y_{k+1} = P^{m}_{y_k}\bigl(\gamma_{k+1}\,\kappa(y_k)\,\nabla_y H(z_k,X_{k+1})\bigr)\),
where \(P^{m}_{y}(v)\) is the negative-entropy mirror (KL) proximal map onto \((0,\infty)^d\cap\{\|y\|_1\le m\}\) (explicitly \(y\mapsto y\odot e^{-v}\) followed by \(\ell_1\)-rescaling if needed).
The paper proves almost sure convergence \(z_k\to z^\star\) and provides an almost sure non-asymptotic bound for the weighted averaged iterates \(\bar z_n := (\sum_{k=1}^n \gamma_k z_{k-1})/(\sum_{k=1}^n \gamma_k)\).

Under suitable regularity and moment assumptions ensuring the SMD recursion \((z_k)_{k\ge 0}\) is well-defined and converges to \(z^\star\), derive an asymptotic distributional error characterization for the stochastic mirror descent risk-budgeting estimator.

Concretely, establish a central limit theorem (CLT) for an appropriately normalized error of either the raw iterates \(z_n\) or the averaged iterates \(\bar z_n\), i.e. prove convergence in distribution of the form
\[a_n\,(\bar z_n - z^\star) \;\Rightarrow\; \mathcal{N}(0,\Sigma),\]
for a deterministic scaling \(a_n\to\infty\) and a covariance matrix \(\Sigma\), and characterize \(a_n\) and \(\Sigma\) in terms of the model primitives \((L,X,b,g,\rho)\) and the mirror map/taming mechanism \((\kappa, P_y^m)\).
open_problem_quote: Potential directions for future research include investigating asymptotic error properties by establishing a central limit theorem for the stochastic MD algorithm presented here.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The problem asks for a universal asymptotic normality theorem under analytical conditions, not for existence/nonexistence on specific finite instances. While simulations can suggest limiting Gaussian behavior, they cannot certify or refute a CLT in general.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: An automated coding loop can explore step-size/averaging/taming variants and empirically estimate asymptotic covariance and scaling on many synthetic models, which can guide conjectures and identify the correct normalization and limiting \(\Sigma\). However, turning this into a rigorous CLT still requires theoretical proof beyond computational search.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The open problem asks for a central limit theorem for the stochastic mirror descent (SMD) estimator \(z_n=(\xi_n,y_n)\) or its weighted average \( ar z_n\) in the risk-budgeting formulation of Arnaiz Iglesias–Cetingoz–Frikha (2024). In that paper, the objective \(h(z)=\mathbb{E}[H(z,X)]\) is strictly convex in \(z\) on \(\mathbb{R}	imes(0,\infty)^d\), but the gradient in \(y\) is singular at the boundary due to \(-\sum_i b_i\log y_i\). The authors introduce the taming factor \(\kappa(y)=(\min_i y_i)\wedge 1\) and a KL/negative-entropy mirror prox map \(P_y^m\) on \(\{y\ge 0,\ \|y\|_1\le m\}\), proving almost sure convergence and an almost sure non-asymptotic bound for \(h( ar z_n)-h(z^\star)\).

No forward-citing papers were provided, so the closest relevant literature is the classical stochastic approximation and averaging theory that typically yields CLTs for first-order stochastic optimization. The key template is Polyak–Juditsky averaging: under local smoothness and stability (invertible Jacobian \(A:=
abla^2 h(z^\star)\) and martingale-difference noise with covariance \(S\)), one expects \(\sqrt{n}( ar z_n-z^\star)\Rightarrow\mathcal{N}(0,\Sigma)\), where \(\Sigma\) solves a Lyapunov equation involving \(A\) and \(S\). Extending this to the present setting requires (i) showing that near \(y^\star\in(0,\infty)^d\) the taming satisfies \(\kappa(y)=1\) and the mirror prox step is differentiable so the recursion reduces locally to a smooth SA; and (ii) controlling the probability of excursions toward the boundary where \(\kappa(y)
eq 1\) and the log-barrier term dominates. General SA references (Kushner–Yin; Benveniste–Métivier–Priouret) provide CLTs for state-dependent gains and constrained recursions, but the specific combination of KL mirror map plus global taming appears not to have a ready-made CLT in the risk-budgeting literature.

Accordingly, the problem appears to remain open in the specific form posed by the paper: a full CLT with explicit \(a_n\) and \(\Sigma\) for \(z_n\) or \( ar z_n\) under the taming/mirror mechanism. A promising direction is to prove a two-stage result: first, establish that after a (random) finite time the iterates enter and remain in a compact subset of \((0,\infty)^d\) where \(\kappa\equiv 1\) and the \(\ell_1\)-constraint is inactive (so \(P_y^m(v)=y\odot e^{-v}\)); second, apply standard SA/averaging CLTs to the resulting locally smooth recursion, yielding \(a_n=\sqrt{n}\) (or \(\sqrt{\sum_{k\le n}\gamma_k}\) for weighted averages) and \(\Sigma\) characterized via \(
abla^2 h(z^\star)\) and the covariance of \(
abla H(z^\star,X)-
abla h(z^\star)\).

sources.title: Problem Complexity and Method Efficiency in Optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces mirror descent and complexity bounds for convex optimization, providing the foundational deterministic framework underlying entropy/KL mirror maps.
sources.connection: Provides the baseline mirror descent methodology; CLTs for SMD typically linearize the recursion around the minimizer and use stochastic approximation theory built on such first-order schemes.

sources.title: A Stochastic Approximation Method
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes stochastic approximation recursions and convergence principles for root-finding under noisy observations.
sources.connection: The SMD recursion in the paper is a stochastic approximation with a non-Euclidean prox step; CLTs for \(z_n\) or Polyak–Ruppert averages are typically proved by adapting Robbins–Monro asymptotic normality theory to mirror maps and constraints.

sources.title: Acceleration of stochastic approximation by averaging
sources.theorem_or_result: Proves asymptotic normality and optimal covariance for averaged stochastic approximation (Polyak–Ruppert averaging) under smoothness and stability conditions.
sources.connection: Directly targets the desired CLT for the weighted averaged iterates \(\bar z_n\) used in the paper; suggests scaling \(a_n=\sqrt{n}\) (or \(\sqrt{\sum\gamma_k}\) for weighted averages) and covariance given by a Lyapunov equation involving the Jacobian \(\nabla^2 h(z^\star)\) and noise covariance.

sources.title: Stochastic Approximation and Recursive Algorithms and Applications
sources.theorem_or_result: Comprehensive treatment of stochastic approximation, including ODE methods, stability, and asymptotic normality/CLTs for SA under various conditions.
sources.connection: Provides general CLT machinery for SA that can be adapted to the paper's tamed-gradient mirror descent by expressing the prox step as a smooth map near \(z^\star\) and verifying local linearization and martingale-difference noise conditions.

sources.title: Adaptive Algorithms and Stochastic Approximations
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops asymptotic theory (including CLTs) for stochastic approximation and adaptive algorithms with martingale noise.
sources.connection: Useful for proving CLTs for \(z_n\) and averaged \(\bar z_n\) when the update includes state-dependent preconditioning/taming \(\kappa(y_k)\) and a mirror map; provides conditions for asymptotic normality via linearization and stable Jacobians.

sources.title: Dynamics of stochastic approximation algorithms
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes the ODE method for stochastic approximation, linking asymptotic behavior to dynamical systems and invariant sets.
sources.connection: Supports the convergence part already proved in the paper and is often a precursor step before proving CLTs (local linearization around stable equilibria).

sources.title: Adaptive Subgradient Methods for Online Learning and Stochastic Optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops adaptive mirror-descent-type methods (AdaGrad) with regret bounds under convexity and stochasticity.
sources.connection: While not a CLT paper, it situates mirror descent with entropy-like geometries in stochastic optimization; techniques for handling unbounded gradients are related in spirit to the paper's taming \(\kappa(y)\).
